title: Calonemorchis uliginosa
intro: Calonemorchis uliginosa, commonly known as the marsh calone, is a species of orchid native to Australia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as respiratory infections and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Calonemorchis uliginosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues.
